data.content_ref: inline:{\"claim\": \"A multi-decadal drought reduced grain yields, precipitating fiscal insolvency in palace economies.\", \"mechanism\": \"Ecological stress depleted agricultural surplus, causing loss of tribute and collapse of centralized redistribution.\", \"scope\": {\"covers\": [\"Mycenaean Greece\",\"Hittite Anatolia\",\"Ugarit\",\"Levantine city‑states\"], \"excludes\": [\"Egypt\"]}, \"forbidden\": [{\"case\": \"Paleo‑hydrological data indicating stable precipitation and Nile flood levels throughout 1200‑1150 BC.\", \"eval\": \"rubric:std-hist\"}], \"prose_notes\": \"Formal abstract: The hypothesis posits a sequential causal chain from prolonged drought to economic breakdown, diverging from hyperarid climate narratives by emphasizing regional groundwater exhaustion.\"}
